refined_fr.refined_fr: Enable measurement and reporting of material throughput in volumetric or mass units per unit time.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates two distinct obligations: (1) the system's capability to measure/report throughput metrics, and (2) the achievement of a customer-defined target. The latter is a success criterion dependent on operational context, training, and external factors beyond system design. The refined FR isolates the system's functional obligation—providing accurate, timely throughput data—which is solution-neutral and testable independent of target achievement. The target itself belongs in acceptance criteria or KPI definitions, not in the FR.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct instrumented trials under representative operating conditions. Measure reported throughput (m³/h or ton/h) against independent ground-truth measurement (e.g., weigh scales, volume surveys, GPS-based tracking). Verify accuracy, latency, and availability of the reporting interface across the agreed evaluation period. Confirm data is accessible to Site Manager and Equipment Operator roles.
sub_frs: Acquire real-time material throughput data (volume or mass) from active work cycles.
Calculate and aggregate throughput metrics (m³/h or ton/h) at operator-selectable time intervals.
Display or export throughput metrics to authorized stakeholders (Site Manager, Equipment Operator) with timestamp and confidence bounds.

candidate_dps: DP1: Acquire instantaneous flow rate via continuous sensing of material stream properties (mass or volume per unit time) and integrate over evaluation period to compute cumulative throughput
DP2: Periodically sample material quantity at fixed time intervals, compute differential changes, and extrapolate to period-level throughput estimate
DP3: Instrument material input and output boundaries with independent accumulation counters (mass or volume), compute net throughput as difference, and normalize by elapsed time
DP4: Establish reference material batches of known quantity, measure batch processing cycle time, and derive throughput as batch quantity divided by cycle duration
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP3: Instrument material input and output boundaries with independent accumulation counters (mass or volume), compute net throughput as difference, and normalize by elapsed time

potential_sources: Input and output counter calibration drift (mitigated by independent sensing paths)
Time synchronization between counters and reporting system (requires explicit clock reference)
Assumption that input and output boundaries are physically distinct and accessible (worksite layout dependency)
coupling_check.notes: DP3 exhibits low functional coupling: input measurement is logically independent of output measurement, enabling separate sensor selection, calibration, and maintenance. The subtraction operation and time normalization are decoupled from sensing hardware. DP1 risks sensor drift over long periods; DP2 introduces sampling bias; DP4 is brittle if batch size or cycle time varies. DP3 supports acceptance criterion verification by providing continuous, boundary-based accountability.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Material input and output streams are physically separable and measurable at distinct locations. Risk: If material is recycled or diverted internally, net throughput may not reflect operational productivity.

Assumption: Measurement accuracy of ±5% (or better) is achievable with available sensing technology. Risk: If worksite environment (dust, moisture, temperature) degrades sensor performance below acceptance threshold, reported throughput will be unreliable.

Assumption: Evaluation period is sufficiently long (≥ 1 shift or agreed baseline) to average transient operational variations. Risk: Short evaluation windows may not capture representative productivity if startup/shutdown or maintenance events skew results.

Assumption: Worksite safety plan permits installation of sensing infrastructure at input/output boundaries without disrupting operations or creating hazards. Risk: If installation is infeasible or deferred, measurement cannot be implemented.

Assumption: Time reference (clock) for normalization is synchronized across input and output measurement systems. Risk: Clock drift or asynchronous reporting will introduce systematic bias in throughput calculation.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine FR to explicitly state: (1) acceptable measurement uncertainty (e.g., ±5% of productivity_target), (2) minimum evaluation period duration, (3) definition of 'material throughput' (net material exiting system, or gross input accounting for losses), and (4) environmental operating range (temperature, humidity, dust) to validate DP3 feasibility. Add constraint: 'Measurement system shall not introduce operational delays or safety hazards.' This will clarify whether DP3 is realizable and inform IM (sensor type, placement, data aggregation module) selection.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Centralized material flow monitoring and throughput computation subsystem that instruments input/output boundaries, maintains independent accumulation counters, and derives net throughput metrics normalized by elapsed time.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Transmit computed net throughput, accumulation counter states, and material flow metrics to external monitoring/logging systems

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receive configuration parameters for counter initialization, boundary definitions, and throughput computation intervals

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Emit alerts if throughput deviates beyond safe operating thresholds or counter anomalies are detected

module_containment_scores.notes: Excellent containment: material flow instrumentation, accumulation logic, and throughput computation are core to NGHE operational monitoring. Counter state management and time-normalized calculations are self-contained. Minor diffusion: SafetyAlert and Telemetry interfaces require external coupling, but these are standard observability/control channels.

Poor containment: Vehicle module typically owns propulsion, navigation, and structural subsystems. Material flow monitoring is a mission-level concern, not a vehicle platform concern. High diffusion: would require Vehicle to expose internal material boundaries and timing logic, violating separation of concerns.

Poor containment: Teleoperation is a control/communication interface layer, not a data processing or instrumentation layer. Placing accumulation counters and throughput computation here conflates operator control with material flow analytics. High diffusion: logic would leak into Vehicle and NGHE Solution.

Weak containment: Safety Services owns alert generation and safety policy enforcement, but not the primary instrumentation and accumulation logic. Throughput computation is a measurement concern, not a safety enforcement concern. Moderate diffusion: would require Safety Services to depend on external counter sources, creating bidirectional coupling.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The DP (material flow instrumentation with independent accumulation counters and net throughput normalization) is fundamentally a mission-level operational monitoring concern. NGHE Solution is the natural owner because it orchestrates material handling workflows and requires real-time throughput visibility for task planning and performance assessment. Accumulation counters and time-normalized metrics are core to NGHE's operational state, not peripheral to it.
containment_risks: If Vehicle subsystem owns the physical sensors at input/output boundaries, NGHE Solution must establish a clear sensor data interface (not listed in provided interfaces) to read raw counts; recommend adding a 'SensorData' or 'MaterialBoundaryData' interface.
Elapsed time computation requires synchronized clock across NGHE Solution and Vehicle; if clock sources diverge, throughput normalization becomes unreliable.

If multiple independent processes (e.g., Teleoperation, Safety Services) attempt to read or reset counters concurrently, race conditions may corrupt accumulation state; recommend centralizing counter access through NGHE Solution with explicit locking or event-driven updates.
SafetyAlert coupling may create circular dependency if Safety Services also monitors throughput thresholds; recommend unidirectional alert emission from NGHE Solution to Safety Services.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: DP3 is well-aligned with NGHE Solution as stated. No revision needed. However, to reduce diffusion risk, explicitly extend the DP to include: 'DP3.1: Define and enforce a single-writer, multi-reader access pattern for accumulation counters to prevent concurrent modification anomalies' and 'DP3.2: Establish a time-synchronized reference clock shared between NGHE Solution and Vehicle for consistent elapsed time measurement.' These refinements improve containment by making counter ownership and timing assumptions explicit within NGHE Solution's boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Acceptance criteria (reported productivity ≥ productivity_target over evaluation period) is directly measurable via NGHE Solution's throughput computation outputs. Telemetry interface enables external validation of counter states and net throughput metrics. Worksite safety plan compliance is verifiable through SafetyAlert interface coupling to Safety Services. Feasibility is high provided sensor data interface (SensorData/MaterialBoundaryData) is explicitly defined and clock synchronization is established.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns material flow instrumentation, accumulation logic, and throughput normalization. Vehicle owns physical sensors at boundaries; NGHE Solution consumes sensor data via interface. Safety Services consumes alerts from NGHE Solution. Teleoperation receives throughput metrics via Telemetry for operator visibility. Ownership boundaries are well-defined and non-overlapping.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP3 (independent accumulation counters at input/output boundaries, net throughput as difference, time normalization) directly and sufficiently addresses FR (enable measurement and reporting of material throughput in volumetric or mass units per unit time). Logic is sound: dual counters eliminate single-point-of-failure risk, difference computation isolates net flow, and elapsed time normalization yields rate metrics. No logical gaps or ambiguities detected.

diffusion_risks: Missing explicit SensorData/MaterialBoundaryData interface: DP3 assumes access to raw sensor counts at input/output boundaries, but no interface is defined between Vehicle (sensor owner) and NGHE Solution (counter logic owner). Recommend adding inbound interface specifying sensor data schema, update frequency, and boundary definitions.

Clock synchronization assumption not formalized: DP3 requires elapsed time measurement; if Vehicle and NGHE Solution use independent clocks, time-normalized throughput becomes unreliable. Recommend explicit DP refinement (DP3.2) defining shared time reference or clock offset correction mechanism.

Concurrent counter access not protected: DP3 does not specify access control for accumulation counters. If Teleoperation, Safety Services, or external processes attempt concurrent reads/resets, race conditions may corrupt state. Recommend explicit DP refinement (DP3.1) defining single-writer, multi-reader pattern or event-driven update semantics.

SafetyAlert circular dependency risk: If Safety Services independently monitors throughput thresholds and emits alerts, bidirectional coupling emerges. Recommend unidirectional alert flow: NGHE Solution detects anomalies and emits SafetyAlert; Safety Services consumes but does not feed back counter logic.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution containment score of 0.92 is justified: material flow instrumentation, accumulation logic, and throughput computation are core mission-level operational concerns, not peripheral. NGHE orchestrates material handling workflows and requires real-time throughput visibility for task planning and performance assessment. Accumulation counters and time-normalized metrics are integral to NGHE's operational state. Minor diffusion (0.08) is acceptable: Telemetry and SafetyAlert are standard observability/control channels, not core logic leakage. Vehicle (0.35 containment, 0.65 diffusion), Teleoperation (0.28/0.72), and Safety Services (0.42/0.58) are correctly identified as poor candidates; placing DP3 in any of these would violate separation of concerns and create high diffusion. Ownership allocation to NGHE Solution is sound.

recommended_revisions: DP3.1 (Counter Access Control): Extend DP3 to explicitly define a single-writer, multi-reader access pattern for accumulation counters. NGHE Solution is the sole writer; Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and external systems are readers. Implement via mutex, event-driven updates, or immutable counter snapshots to prevent concurrent modification anomalies.

DP3.2 (Time Synchronization): Extend DP3 to establish a time-synchronized reference clock shared between NGHE Solution and Vehicle. Define clock source (e.g., GPS, NTP, Vehicle system clock), acceptable offset tolerance, and correction mechanism. Ensure elapsed time measurement is consistent across subsystems.

DP3.3 (Sensor Data Interface): Add explicit inbound interface from Vehicle to NGHE Solution specifying raw sensor count schema, update frequency, boundary definitions (e.g., inlet vs. outlet), and sensor reliability metadata. This formalizes the DP<->IM bridge and reduces ambiguity about sensor ownership and data flow.

DP3.4 (Alert Unidirectionality): Clarify that SafetyAlert is unidirectional: NGHE Solution emits alerts to Safety Services when throughput deviates beyond thresholds or counter anomalies are detected. Safety Services does not feed back counter reset or recalibration commands to NGHE Solution; such requests are routed through Tasking interface only.

Acceptance Criteria Refinement: Specify how productivity_target is computed from throughput metrics (e.g., net mass per hour, volume per hour). Define evaluation period duration and sampling frequency. Clarify whether target is instantaneous rate or cumulative over period. This ensures Telemetry output format aligns with acceptance criteria verification.

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass logically, confirming that DP3 (independent accumulation counters at input/output boundaries with net throughput and time normalization) is sound and correctly allocated to NGHE Solution. However, four critical diffusion risks and one acceptance criteria ambiguity have been identified that must be resolved before proceeding to sub-level decomposition. These risks are not logical gaps in the DP itself, but rather missing formalization of interface contracts, concurrency semantics, time synchronization, and alert directionality that are essential for safe and reliable implementation. Given high safety criticality, these refinements must be captured as explicit DP sub-requirements (DP3.1–DP3.4) at the current level before deriving lower-level FRs. Proceeding to sub-level decomposition without these refinements would propagate ambiguity into detailed design and increase integration risk.

revise.why: DP3 requires four explicit sub-refinements to address diffusion risks and formalize interface contracts: (1) DP3.1 defines single-writer, multi-reader access control for accumulation counters to prevent race conditions; (2) DP3.2 establishes shared time reference and clock synchronization mechanism between NGHE Solution and Vehicle; (3) DP3.3 formalizes inbound SensorData/MaterialBoundaryData interface schema, update frequency, and boundary definitions; (4) DP3.4 clarifies unidirectional SafetyAlert flow and prevents circular coupling with Safety Services. Additionally, acceptance criteria must be refined to specify productivity_target computation method (mass/volume per unit time), evaluation period duration, sampling frequency, and Telemetry output format alignment. These refinements are DP-level concerns (design logic and interface formalization), not FR-level decomposition, and must be resolved before sub-level FR derivation.

assumptions: NGHE Solution has exclusive write access to accumulation counters; all other subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are read-only consumers.
Vehicle owns physical sensors at material boundaries and provides raw sensor counts via a well-defined interface; NGHE Solution does not directly access hardware sensors.
A shared time reference (GPS, NTP, or Vehicle system clock) is available and can be synchronized with acceptable offset tolerance (to be specified in DP3.2).
SafetyAlert is unidirectional: NGHE Solution emits alerts to Safety Services; Safety Services does not issue counter reset or recalibration commands back to NGHE Solution.
Productivity_target is a static or dynamically updated threshold expressed in consistent units (e.g., kg/hour, m³/hour) and is provided to NGHE Solution via Tasking interface or configuration.
Telemetry interface supports periodic or event-driven throughput metric reporting at a frequency sufficient for operator visibility and acceptance criteria verification.
open_risks: Concurrent counter access anomaly: If multiple subsystems attempt simultaneous reads or if counter reset is triggered during active accumulation, state corruption or metric inconsistency may occur. DP3.1 must define mutex, event-driven snapshot, or immutable counter semantics to mitigate.

Clock synchronization failure: If Vehicle and NGHE Solution clocks drift beyond acceptable tolerance, elapsed time measurement becomes unreliable and throughput normalization yields incorrect rates. DP3.2 must specify clock source, offset tolerance, and correction mechanism; monitoring and alerting for clock skew should be considered.

Sensor data interface ambiguity: Raw sensor count schema, update frequency, boundary definitions (inlet vs. outlet), and sensor reliability metadata are not yet formalized. Missing interface specification increases risk of misalignment between Vehicle sensor output and NGHE Solution counter input logic.

SafetyAlert circular coupling: If Safety Services independently monitors throughput and issues counter recalibration commands, bidirectional coupling emerges, complicating state management and creating potential for conflicting updates. DP3.4 must enforce unidirectionality.

Acceptance criteria verification gap: Productivity_target computation method, evaluation period duration, and sampling frequency are not specified. Telemetry output format may not align with acceptance criteria, delaying or complicating verification.

High safety criticality context: Material throughput measurement is a safety-critical operational metric. Errors in counter logic, time synchronization, or alert generation could lead to incorrect task planning, material overflow/underflow, or delayed safety responses. All DP3 sub-refinements must be validated against safety requirements.
evidence_needed: DP3.1 formalization: Specify access control pattern (mutex, event-driven, immutable snapshots) with pseudocode or state machine diagram showing single-writer, multi-reader semantics and race condition prevention.

DP3.2 formalization: Define clock source (GPS, NTP, Vehicle system clock), acceptable offset tolerance (e.g., ±100 ms), synchronization frequency, and correction mechanism (e.g., linear interpolation, leap second handling). Include clock skew monitoring and alerting logic.

DP3.3 interface specification: Document SensorData/MaterialBoundaryData interface schema (sensor count data type, boundary identifiers, timestamp format), update frequency (e.g., 10 Hz, 100 Hz), sensor reliability metadata (e.g., accuracy, failure modes), and error handling (e.g., missing or out-of-order samples).

DP3.4 clarification: Provide sequence diagram or state machine showing unidirectional SafetyAlert flow: NGHE Solution detects anomalies → emits SafetyAlert → Safety Services consumes. Confirm that Safety Services does not issue counter reset/recalibration commands; such requests are routed through Tasking interface only.

Acceptance criteria refinement: Specify productivity_target as a function of throughput metrics (e.g., net mass per hour = (output_count - input_count) / elapsed_time_hours). Define evaluation period (e.g., 1 hour, 8 hours, 24 hours), sampling frequency for Telemetry reporting, and pass/fail threshold (e.g., reported_productivity ≥ 0.95 × productivity_target).

Safety validation: Confirm that DP3 sub-refinements (counter access control, time synchronization, sensor interface, alert directionality) satisfy safety requirements for material throughput measurement in high-criticality context. Document hazard analysis and mitigation strategies.
